mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and pushes investors out the risk curve into equities, producing persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon while ZIRP persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of 2011-10-01, near zero lower bound since Dec 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during the ZIRP period, or forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a two-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises consumer income expectations and corporate earnings outlooks, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE declined from 9.0% (2011-10-01 vintage) to 8.8% revised, indicating improvement
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time or show no statistically significant difference from non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized broadening of consumer and residential investment demand that is not captured by either series alone; this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because it reduces double-dip recession fears.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 658.0 units (2011-09-01), RSAFS vintage 397666 million (2011-10-01)
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to outperform single-series (RSAFS-only or HOUST-only) triggers by at least 2 percentage points in mean 20-day forward return, indicating no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production during a post-crisis recovery regime signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, but this relationship weakens or reverses in late-cycle overheating regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 94.1851 (2011-09-01) rising from crisis trough
failure_condition: During designated post-crisis recovery windows, correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) across at least two historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly nonfarm payroll growth reported on release day generates an immediate short-horizon rally in equities as it resolves uncertainty about labor market momentum, with the effect concentrated in the first week following the release.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 131516 thousand (2011-10-01) reflecting monthly job gains
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates above a 3% year-over-year threshold while the federal funds rate remains near zero, markets price in rising odds of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 45% of cases, or mean triggered return is not significantly lower than baseline mean return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales signals a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand that individually weak readings in either series do not fully capture, raising recession probability and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage 13352.8 (2011-07-01) reflecting sluggish post-crisis growth
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to show mean 60-day forward returns at least 3 percentage points lower than non-triggered periods, or GDPC1-only signal performs equally well without RSAFS confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the unemployment rate remains elevated above 8% but the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, the market interprets persistent labor slack as a signal of continued accommodative policy, creating a 'bad news is good news' dynamic that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.8% revised alongside FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of late 2011
regime_hint: high unemployment with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE > 8% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or the sign flips to negative in more than 45% of instances.
